title: Conservation Scientists
description: Manage, improve, and protect natural resources to maximize their use without damaging the environment. May conduct soil surveys and develop plans to eliminate soil erosion or to protect rangelands. May instruct farmers, agricultural production managers, or ranchers in best ways to use crop rotation, contour plowing, or terracing to conserve soil and water; in the number and kind of livestock and forage plants best suited to particular ranges; and in range and farm improvements, such as fencing and reservoirs for stock watering.

tasks.text: Apply principles of specialized fields of science, such as agronomy, soil science, forestry, or agriculture, to achieve conservation objectives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan soil management or conservation practices, such as crop rotation, reforestation, permanent vegetation, contour plowing, or terracing, to maintain soil or conserve water.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor projects during or after construction to ensure projects conform to design specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise land users, such as farmers or ranchers, on plans, problems, or alternative conservation solutions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Implement soil or water management techniques, such as nutrient management, erosion control, buffers, or filter strips, in accordance with conservation plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compute design specifications for implementation of conservation practices, using survey or field information, technical guides or engineering manuals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Gather information from geographic information systems (GIS) databases or applications to formulate land use recommendations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate on work teams to plan, develop, or implement programs or policies for improving environmental habitats, wetlands, or groundwater or soil resources.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compute cost estimates of different conservation practices, based on needs of land users, maintenance requirements, or life expectancy of practices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop or maintain working relationships with local government staff or board members.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Revisit land users to view implemented land use practices or plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Visit areas affected by erosion problems to identify causes or determine solutions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide information, knowledge, expertise, or training to government agencies at all levels to solve water or soil management problems or to assure coordination of resource protection activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Enter local soil, water, or other environmental data into adaptive or Web-based decision tools to identify appropriate analyses or techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze results of investigations to determine measures needed to maintain or restore proper soil management.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop, conduct, or participate in surveys, studies, or investigations of various land uses to inform corrective action plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate or implement technical, financial, or administrative assistance programs for local government units to ensure efficient program implementation or timely responses to requests for assistance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to complaints or questions on wetland jurisdiction, providing information or clarification.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile or interpret biodata to determine extent or type of wetlands or to aid in program formulation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review or approve amendments to comprehensive local water plans or conservation district plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review proposed wetland restoration easements or provide technical recommendations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop soil maps.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Manage field offices or involve staff in cooperative ventures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Initiate, schedule, or conduct annual audits or compliance checks of program implementation by local government.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Identify or recommend integrated weed and pest management (IPM) strategies, such as resistant plants, cultural or behavioral controls, soil amendments, insects, natural enemies, barriers, or pesticides.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review annual reports of counties, conservation districts, or watershed management organizations, certifying compliance with mandated reporting requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review grant applications or make funding recommendations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or conduct environmental studies, such as plant material field trials or wildlife habitat impact studies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct fact-finding or mediation sessions among government units, landowners, or other agencies to resolve disputes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop water conservation or harvest plans, using weather information systems, irrigation information management systems, or other sources of daily evapotranspiration (ET) data.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
